[user]tell me a story about a molecule.
[Ninereeds]First, Jack sits in science class and studies the water molecule drawn on the board. The diagram shows one oxygen atom connected to two hydrogen atoms. Jack asks, "Does every molecule have more than one kind of atom?" The teacher nods and explains that a molecule needs at least two atoms bonded together.

The teacher points to a chart that lists different elements. Jack understands that each element has a unique kind of atom. If two hydrogen atoms bond with one oxygen atom, then the water molecule forms. Jack writes this rule in his notebook.

After the lesson, the teacher hands out molecule models for the class. Jack picks up a model with one carbon atom and four hydrogen atoms. He studies how the atoms join at specific angles. "If we change the number of atoms, will the molecule become different?" Jack asks. The teacher replies that each molecule has a unique structure based on its atoms.

Finally, Jack looks around the classroom and realizes that every object is made of molecules. The wooden desk, the glass window, and the air all contain atoms bonded together. He understands that chemistry is the study of how different elements combine.
